---
title: "Muhammad_Ahmad_Vignette"
author: "Muhammad Ahmad"
date: "2026-04-11"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(naniar)
```

```{r}
url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/MuhammadAhmad0006/SPRING2026TIDYVERSE/refs/heads/main/M.Ahmad_Coffe_sales.csv"

coffeeRevenue <- read_csv(url)

coffeeRevenue
```

```{r}
glimpse(coffeeRevenue)
```

```{r}
vis_miss(coffeeRevenue)
```

```{r}
monthly <- coffeeRevenue |>
  mutate(Date = ymd(Date),
         month = floor_date(Date, "month")) |>
  group_by(month) |>
  summarise(revenue = sum(money), .groups = "drop")

monthly
```

Monthly revenue time span
```{r}
ggplot(monthly, aes(x = month, y = revenue)) + 
       geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
       geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Monthly Revenue",
    subtitle = "March 2024 – March 2025",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Revenue ($)",
    caption = "Source: your data"
  )
```
